notes:
cắn = to bite, when a person or animal uses their teeth on something.  

It’s a verb, often used with animals or insects (dogs, mosquitoes, ants), and also with people in a physical sense. You’ll often hear it in the passive form with bị when someone is the victim.  

It can also be figurative: cắn răng (grit your teeth = force yourself to endure) or cắn môi (bite your lip, often from emotion or concentration).  

Close word: đớp (to snap/bite quickly, usually animals, more sudden and rough).
exampleSentences: Em đừng đưa tay lại gần con chó vì nó có thể cắn.
Anh nhỏ tuổi hơn, nhưng rất sợ bị kiến cắn khi đi chơi ngoài vườn.
Chị có thể cho bé thử cắn một miếng táo này được không?
exampleSentencesTranslated: Don't put your hand near the dog because it might bite.
He is younger, but he is very afraid of being bite by ants when playing in the garden.
Can you let the child try to bite a piece of this apple?

example.en: The dog bit me when I got close.
example.es: El perro me mordió cuando me acerqué.
example.vi: Con chó cắn tôi khi tôi đến gần.

definitions.en: To bite; to use the teeth to cut into something.
definitions.es: Morder; usar los dientes para cortar algo.
definitions.vi: Cắn; sử dụng răng để cắt vào cái gì đó.
